title: NetBIOS
description: Network Basic Input/Output System — provides session services for Windows networks.
fullExplanation: NetBIOS (Network Basic Input/Output System) is an API that provides session-layer services for Windows-based networks. It allows applications on different computers to communicate over a LAN. NetBIOS operates over TCP/IP (NBT) using ports 137 (UDP name service), 138 (UDP datagram), and 139 (TCP session). It provides three services: Name Service (resolving NetBIOS names to IPs), Datagram Service (connectionless messaging), and Session Service (connection-oriented communication).
keyFacts: Resolves NetBIOS names (up to 15 chars) to IP addresses
Predecessor to DNS for Windows name resolution
NetBIOS over TCP/IP (NBT) defined in RFC 1001/1002
Largely replaced by DNS and Direct Hosting (port 445)
Still used in legacy Windows environments

versions.feature: IBM PC Network, LAN-only, no routing
